summary: Scrutiny failed because the manager fix in commit 3ea1db2 now clears previously active MCP servers after a strict `connect_all()` failure, which diverges from the Python source-of-truth behavior the feature was supposed to preserve while adding MCP lifecycle coverage.

issues.description: `MCPServerManager::connect_all()` now clears `active_servers` on any strict connect failure, so a manager that already had active servers loses that prior active set after a later strict retry fails. The Python source-of-truth preserves the previous active servers in this case, so VAL-MCP-001 is not fully met.
issues.evidence: Rust clears state at crates/agents-core/src/mcp/manager.rs:84-86. The Python source preserves previous state at reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/mcp/manager.py:192-221, and the upstream test explicitly asserts the behavior at reference/openai-agents-python/tests/mcp/test_mcp_server_manager.py:372-386.

codeReview.summary: The feature adds useful MCP coverage for strict cleanup, resources, tool filtering/meta resolution, and streamed runner metadata, but it also regresses strict manager lifecycle semantics for already-active servers.

issues.description: On strict connect failure, the new error path cleans up and then clears `active_servers`, which erases the previous active set instead of restoring it per upstream behavior.

commands.observation: Reviewed commit metadata and confirmed the feature changed four files: `manager.rs`, `server.rs`, `util.rs`, and `mcp_semantics.rs`.
Reviewed the full diff and identified the new strict-failure branch in `MCPServerManager::connect_all()` that clears active state after cleanup.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... print numbered excerpts from Rust and Python MCP manager files plus the upstream preserving-active-servers test ... PY
commands.observation: Captured exact line evidence showing the Rust clear-state path versus the Python logic that restores `previous_active_servers` and the upstream test that requires that preservation.
